coredata.dc:title: A novel gold nanosol SERS quantitative analysis method for trace Na+ based on carbon dot catalysis
coredata.dc:creator: Li, Chongning
Wang, Haidong
Luo, Yanghe
Wen, Guiqing
Jiang, Zhiliang
coredata.dc:description: The Ca-doped carbon dots (CDCa) exhibited strong catalysis of HAuCl4-H2O2 reaction to produce gold nanoparticle (AuNP), using Vitoria blue 4R (VB4r) as molecular probe, it appeared a strong surface enhanced Raman scattering (SERS) peak at 1615 cm−1 in the AuNP nanosol substrate. When catalyst CDCa increased, the SERS peak enhanced linearly owing to AuNPs increasing, that is, the AuNP concentration is linear to SERS signal. Potassium pyroantimonate (PA) ligand can adsorb on CDCa surface to inhibit the catalysis and cause the SERS peak decreasing. The analyte of Na+ reacts with PA to produce the stable precipitate of [Na2(PA)] and free CDCa catalyst. The more Na+, the more CDCa released, and the stronger SERS signal due to more AuNP substrate. Accordingly, a SERS quantitative analysis method for Na+ was developed based on CDCa catalytic SERS reaction, with a linear range of 0.004–0.043 μmol/L, and a detection limit of 0.0019 μmol/L Na.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Na+
Potassium pyroantimonate
Ca-doped carbon dots
Nanocatalysis
SERS

xocs:item-toc-entry.xocs:item-toc-section-title: Preparation of the Ca-doped carbon quantum dot (CDCa)
Preparation of the N-doped carbon quantum dot (CDN)

xocs:funding.xocs:funding-agency: National Natural Science Foundation of China

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: Principle of the SERS detection of Na+ based on CD catalysis of AuNP reaction.

SERS spectra of CDCa-HAuCl4-H2O2-VB4r system. a:1.13 mol/L CH3CH2OH + 0.193 mmol/L HAuCl4 + 3.3 mmol/L H2O2 + 0.33 μmol/L VB4r b:a + 0.03 ng/mL CDCa; c:a + 0.1 ng/mL CDCa; d:a + 0.2 ng/mL CDCa; e:a + 0.33 ng/mL CDCa; f:a + 0.67 ng/mL CDCa.

SERS spectra of Na+-PA-CDCa-HAuCl4-H2O2-VB4r system. a:1.13 mol/L CH3CH2OH + 3.14 μmol/L PA + 0.193 mmol/L HAuCl4 + 3.3 mmol/L H2O2 + 0.67 ng/mL CDCa + 0.33 μmol/L VB4r; b:a + 0.004 μmol/L L Na+; c:a + 0.009 μmol/L Na+; d:a + 0.017 μmol/L Na+; e:a + 0.024 μmol/L Na+; f:a + 0.034 μmol/L Na+; g:a + 0.043 μmol/L Na+.
SEM of the analytical system. a:1.13 mol/L CH3CH2OH + 3.14 μmol/L PA + 0.193 mmol/L HAuCl4 + 3.3 mmol/L H2O2 + 0.67 ng/mL CDCa; b:a + 8.6 μmol/L Na+; c:a + 17.2 μmol/L Na+.
Comparing of the catalytic characteristics of nanoenzymes-HAuCl4-H2O2-VB4r system.
Analytical features for nanocatalytic SERS detection of Na+.

head.ce:title: A novel gold nanosol SERS quantitative analysis method for trace Na+ based on carbon dot catalysis

ce:affiliation.ce:label: a
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Key Laboratory of Ecology of Rare and Endangered Species and Environmental Protection (Guangxi Normal University), Ministry of Education, Guangxi Key Laboratory of Environmental Pollution Control Theory and Technology, Guilin 541004, China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: Key Laboratory of Ecology of Rare and Endangered Species and Environmental Protection (Guangxi Normal University)
Guangxi Key Laboratory of Environmental Pollution Control Theory and Technology

ce:affiliation.ce:label: b
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: School of Food and Bioengineering, Hezhou University, Hezhou 542899, China

ce:correspondence.ce:text: Corresponding authors at: Key Laboratory of Ecology of Rare and Endangered Species and Environmental Protection (Guangxi Normal University), Ministry of Education, Guangxi Key Laboratory of Environmental Pollution Control Theory and Technology, Guilin 541004, China (Z. Jiang).

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: Key Laboratory of Ecology of Rare and Endangered Species and Environmental Protection (Guangxi Normal University)
Guangxi Key Laboratory of Environmental Pollution Control Theory and Technology

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: The Ca-doped carbon dots (CDCa) exhibited strong catalysis of HAuCl4-H2O2 reaction to produce gold nanoparticle (AuNP), using Vitoria blue 4R (VB4r) as molecular probe, it appeared a strong surface enhanced Raman scattering (SERS) peak at 1615 cm−1 in the AuNP nanosol substrate. When catalyst CDCa increased, the SERS peak enhanced linearly owing to AuNPs increasing, that is, the AuNP concentration is linear to SERS signal. Potassium pyroantimonate (PA) ligand can adsorb on CDCa surface to inhibit the catalysis and cause the SERS peak decreasing. The analyte of Na+ reacts with PA to produce the stable precipitate of [Na2(PA)] and free CDCa catalyst. The more Na+, the more CDCa released, and the stronger SERS signal due to more AuNP substrate. Accordingly, a SERS quantitative analysis method for Na+ was developed based on CDCa catalytic SERS reaction, with a linear range of 0.004–0.043 μmol/L, and a detection limit of 0.0019 μmol/L Na.

ce:section.ce:para: Nanoenzyme is a kind of artificial enzyme that was used to describe gold clusters with significant ribonuclease activity, which first proposed by Manea, Houillion, Pasquato, and Scrimin (2004). Nanoenzymes not only have highly catalytic activity as natural enzymes, but also have the stable and economical properties as mimic enzymes, and have been used in different fields such as material science, physics, chemistry, biology and environmental science (Gao et al., 2007; Karakoti, Singh, & Dowding, 2010; Yan, Ge, Liu, & Ouyang, 2006; Asuri, Karajanagi, Dordick, & Kane, 2006). In nanoanalysis, the metal nanoenzyme was used in spectrophotometry. Xiong et al. (2015) demonstrated that the as-synthesized magnetic nanoflowers Fe3O4@MnO2 possess intrinsic oxidase-like activity and can catalytically oxidize 4-aminoantipyrine (4-AAP) and phenol to produce pink color products, and a simple colorimetric method for 1.0–120 μM phenol was developed with a detection limit of 0.15 μM. Wei and Wang (2008) utilized Fe3O4 MNPs as peroxidase mimetics to detect 0.005–0.1 mM H2O2 and 0.05–1 mM glucose spectrophotometrically. We have known that metal nanoparticle or metal nanozyme, especially its nanosol, is easy pollution and unstable. Thus, it is significant to explore new and green nonmetal nanoenzyme such as carbon dots.

Carbon dots (C-dots) are a new type of carbon nanomaterials, and have been paid much attention for outstanding stability, low toxicity, ease of functionalization, and have been applied to analytical chemistry. For example, Shi et al. (2011) found carbon nanodots (C-Dots) possess intrinsic peroxidase-like activity, and catalytically oxidize 3,3′,5,5′-tetramethylbenzidine (TMB) by H2O2 to produce a blue product. Combining with bioenzyme, a 0.001–0.50 mM glucose can be determined spectrophotometrically. In fluorescence analysis, a main work is improved the fluorescence quantum yield of C-dots by dopant (Norris, Efros, & Erwin, 2008; Wang et al., 2014; Du & Guo, 2016). Nitrogen-doped C-dots were ease of preparation, exhibit excellent fluorescence properties, and have been used in biosensing and environmental detection (Wang, Niu, He, & Cai, 2016; Wu, Jiang, Gu, & Tong, 2017; Li, Yu, & You, 2015; Han et al., 2017; Liu, Wang, Cheng, & Liu, 2016; Gong et al., 2015). Gong et al. (2015) synthesized a N-doped carbon dots (N-CDs) by microwave-assisted pyrolysis of chitosan, acetic acid and N-dopant of 1,2-ethylenediamine. Based on N-CDs fluorescence quenching by Fe3+, a 0.010–1.8 ppm Fe3+ can be detected by fluorescence method. Up to date, there is rare report about using CD as catalyst to amplify Raman signal. Surface enhanced Raman scattering (SERS) is a Raman scattering signal enhancement phenomenon that some molecules adsorb on the surface of metal nanoparticle (Anderson & Moskovits, 2006; Baruah & Miller, 2017; Mosier-Boss, 2017; Kaleem, Azmat, Sharma, Shen, & Ding, 2019). It can detect sensitively the concentration of molecules adsorbed to the nanosurface and give abundant molecular structure information (Liang et al., 2015; Ma et al., 2013; Li, Fan, Liang, Liu, & Jiang, 2018; Wang et al., 2017), and has been used to detect heavy metals. Due to very small Raman scattering section of alkali metal such as sodium ion, there are no reports about the SERS quantitative analytical method for sodium ion.

Sodium ion (Na+) is an important physiological element. Along with K+, it plays an important role in maintaining cell metabolism, regulating osmotic pressure, maintaining acid-base balance and maintaining cellular emergency functions. At the same time, Na+ plays different roles in biological growth process, including neurotransmission, enzyme activation, apoptosis, regulating of blood pressure and the concentration of other ions in cells. The imbalance of Na+ concentrations between intracellular and extracellular fluids has always been associated with a series of diseases including convulsions, hypotension, vomiting, heart and renal failure. Therefore, the detection of Na+ has become an important issue. At present, the common methods for Na+ are potentiometric titration (Ma, 2015), flame atomic emission spectrometry (Oliveira, Villa, & Antunes, 2009), temperature titration (Smith & Haider, 2016), atomic absorption spectrometry (Liu, 2012), ion chromatography (Zhang, Wang, & Lu, 2012), and inductively coupled plasma mass spectrometry (Wang et al., 2014). Some of these methods have the disadvantages of not-high sensitivity, complicated operation, high-cost, more interference, and many influencing factors. In this paper, the as-prepared CDCa was used as nanocatalyst, and a rapid and sensitive SERS method was proposed for determination of Na+, coupled with the PA reaction.

A model of DXR smart Raman spectrometer (Thermo, USA) was used to record SERS spectra by single scanning and intensity, with laser wavelength of 633 nm, power of 3.5 mW, slit of 50 μm and acquisition time of 5 s. A model of TU-1901 double beam UV–visible spectrophotometer (Beijing Purkinje General Instrument Co., Ltd., Beijing, China) was used record absorption spectra. A model of Zetasizer Nano nanometer particle size and zeta potential analyzer (Malvern, UK) and a model of S-4800 field emission scanning electron microscope (Hitachi High-Technologies Corporation, Japan/Oxford company, UK) were used.

A 11.8 mmol/L potassium pyroantimonate (PA) solution: 1.5 g of potassium pyroantimonate (K2H2SbO7·4H2O, Shanghai Reagent Four Hewei Chemical Co., Ltd., Shanghai, China) was added into a conical flask containing 150 mL water, and 0.15 g KOH were added and heat to boiling to dissolve. After cooling to room temperature, the solution was transferred into a 250 mL volumetric flask and diluted to the mark with water. Before use, it was diluted 100 times with water. A 2.9 mmol/L HAuCl4·4H2O (National Pharmaceutical Group Chemical Reagents Company, Shanghai, China) was used to prepare nanogold. A 10 μmol/L VB4r, 129 mmol/L NaCl, 10 mol/L H2O2, 50% CH3CH2OH, 50 mmol/L KOH and 129 mmol/L NaCl solution were prepared. A 39.33 μmol/L sodium pyroantimonate solution was prepared as follows: 118 μmol/L potassium pyroantimonate solution and 129 mmol/L NaCl solution were mixed in a volume ratio of 1:2 according to the desired volume. Three kinds of graphene quantum dots (No. XF075, horizontal size: 5–15 nm, thickness: 1–2 nm, layer number: 1–5, carbon content: 80%–90%, Oxygen/nitrogen content: 10/20%; graphene quantum dot with blue light (GQDb), No. XF042; graphene quantum dot with yellow light (GQDy), No. XF074, thickness: 1–2 nm, layer number: 1–5, horizontal size: 5–15 nm, carbon content: 60%, oxygen/nitrogen content 40%) and a kind of nano-silica (SiO2, KH550 treatment) were purchased from Nanjing XFN Materials Technology Co, Ltd. A 10 µg/mL graphene oxide (GO), 0.02 mg/mL GQDb, 0.02 mg/mL GQDy, 0.2 g/L fullerene (C60), 2 g/L fullerenol (C60-OH), 0.5 mg/mL SiO2, 1.25 mg/mL MoS2 were used. All reagents were of analytical grade and the water was doubly distilled.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Preparation of the Ca-doped carbon quantum dot (CDCa)
ce:section.ce:para: A 1 g citric acid were added into a 100 mL polytetrafluoroethylene tank reactor containing 10 mL water and mixed to dissolve. Then a 200 mg Ca(OH)2 was added and 500 μL ethylenediamine was added slowly. Mixing well, the reactor was heated at 200 °C for 4 h in a muffle furnace. After cooling, the brown product was dissolved in10 mL water to obtain 0.1 g/L CDCa solution. Finally, the solution was centrifuged at 10,000 rpm for 10 min, and the supernatant was adjusted to pH 7.5 before use.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Preparation of the N-doped carbon quantum dot (CDN)
ce:section.ce:para: Under the ultrasonic condition, 1 g citric acid and 1 g urea were dissolved in 30 mL water to form transparent brown solution. Then the solution was poured into the high pressure kettle with polytetrafluoroethylene substrate. After the kettle sealed, it was heated at 180 °C for 5 h. After reaction finished, the mixture was naturally cooled to room temperature to obtain the brown clarified solution. To purify CDN, the solution was subjected to dialysis against water 10 h through a dialysate bag with 3500 Da molecular weight. It was neutralized by NaOH solution before use. The final concentration of CDN solution was 0.021 g/mL, and then dilute to the desired concentration before use.

A 100 μL 50% CH3CH2OH, 100 μL 118 μmol/L PA and a certain amount of NaCl standard solutions were added into a 5 mL marked test tube and mixed well. After 10 min, a 100 μL 10 μg/L CDCa, 100 μL 2.9 mmol/L HAuCl4 and 50 μL 0.1 mmoL/L H2O2 solutions were added, and diluted to 1.5 mL with water. The mixture was placed in a 60 °C water bath for reacting 25 min and the reaction was terminated by tap-water. A 50 μL 10 μmol/L VB4r was added and the mixture was transferred into a 1 cm quartz cell, and its SERS spectra were recorded by the single scanning the sample solution from 200 to 1700 cm−1. The SERS intensity I at 1615 cm−1 for the Na+ and the blank I0 without Na+ were measured, and the ΔI = I − I0 was calculated.

The SERS signal is depended not only on the concentration of the nanoparticles including size, shape and the distribution of the nanoparticles, but also on the molecular probe concentration. In general, it is difficult to SERS quantitative analysis in solid substrate because there are many influence factors. Although some SERS quantitative analysis methods on solid substrate were reported by internal standard procedure with high sensitivity and wide dynamic detection range, the operation was complicated and slope of working curve is small with low accuracy. Using reproducible nanosol as substrate, the SERS quantitative analysis may be developed for determination of SERS molecular probes, based on the linear relationship between SERS signal and the probe concentration. Recently, our research group has found that the SERS signal is also linear to nanosol concentration when molecular probes held constant. Furthermore, some gold and silver particle nanoreaction have been studied by SERS detection technique using sensitive SERS molecular probe such as Vitoria blue B (VBB) and VB4r, and coupled with immunoreactions and aptamer reaction to develop some highly sensitive and selective SERS quantitative analysis methods, that were used the formed nanoparticle from nanocatalytic indicator reaction as sol substrate, this is a new type of SERS quantitative analysis method based-nanosol substrate changing (Liang et al., 2015; Li et al., 2018). Similarly to molecular kinetic analytical principle, the product of nanoparticles such as AuNPs in the nanocatalytic particle system is linear to nanocatalyst concentration, thus, it was coupled with the selective biochemical reaction to develop a SERS quantitative analysis method for target.

Under the selected conditions of 1.13 mol/L CH3CH2OH-3.14 μmol/L PA-0.193 mmol/L HAuCl4-3.3 mmol/L H2O2-0.67 ng/mL CDCa-0.33 μmol/L VB4r at 60 °C for reaction of 25 min, the reaction of HAuCl4 and H2O2 was very slow to produce AuNP. When nanocatalyst such as CDCa was added, the AuNP reaction enhanced greatly in the catalytic system. The produced AuNP concentration in the catalytic system increased with CDCa concentration increasing. Upon addition of a constant concentration of VB4r molecule probes, there is a strongest SERS peak appeared at 1615 cm−1, that was ascribing to tensile vibration of CN and CC–CC, and the SERS peak intensity increased linearly with CDCa concentration increasing due to the AuNP nanosol substrate concentration increasing. Upon addition of PA, it binds on CDCa surface to inhibit the catalysis of CDCa to produce AuNPs, and the SERS peak intensity decreased due to formation of AuNPs decreasing. When analyte of Na+ was added, it reacts with PA to form stable Na2PA complexes, to release free CDCa with catalysis. The Na+ concentration increased, the released catalyst of CDCa increased, the product of AuNP increased, and the SERS peak intensity enhanced linearly when the added VB4r concentration held constant with 0.33 μmol/L. Based on this ground, a new SERS quantitative analysis method was proposed for detection of Na+ (Fig. 1 ). In this article, the concentration of CDCa and Na+ is all linear to the SERS peak intensity, and the latter can be utilized to determine real sample. This work was used nanosol substrate as indicator to develop SERS quantitative analytical method, and was different to the molecular probe.

According to the SERS procedure, the UV–vis absorption spectra of analytical systems were recorded. There was a surface plasmon resonance (SPR) absorption peak at 550 nm of the Na+-PA-CDCa-H2O2-HAuCl4 system, and the peak gradually increased and narrowed with the increase of Na+ concentration in the range of 0–6.88 μmol/L, the peak wavelength was shift-red from 535 nm to 555 nm and the half peak width changed from 110 nm to 94 nm (Fig. S2). The peak at 550 nm is ascribing to the surface Plasmon resonance absorption of produced AuNPs from HAuCl4-H2O2 reaction.

Under the experimental conditions, the carbon nanoparticles, including GO, CDCa, CDN, GODs, GQDy, GQDb, C60 and C60-OH, all catalyze the reaction of HAuCl4-H2O2 to form gold nanoparticles. As an example of CDCa, in the range of 0.03–0.67 ng/mL CDCa, the catalytic ability enhanced with the catalyst concentration increasing, the produced AuNPs increased, and the SERS peak intensity at 1615 cm−1 increased linearly when the added VB4r molecule probe concentration was 0.33 μmol/L. At the same time, the catalytic effects of SiO2 and MoS2 were also examined. When the concentration of GO, CDCa, CDN, GODs, GQDy, GQDb, C60, C60-OH, SiO2 and MoS2 is in the linear range of Table 1 respectively, the SERS peak intensity at enhanced linearly. From Table 1, we can see that the catalysis of CDCa is stronger than other nanoparticles due to CDCa surface containing rich surface electrons with the donor electron element of Ca.

According to the selected conditions, 0.1 mL reaction solution was taken and diluted 100 times in a 10 mL centrifuge tube. A 10 μL sample solution was dropped onto the silicon wafer, naturally dried, and the SEM was recorded. For the Na+-PA-CDCa-HAuCl4-H2O2-VB4r system, the binding of CDCa and PA inhibit the catalysis when there was no Na+. So the reaction of H2O2 reducing HAuCl4 at 60 °C was slow, and the produced AuNP was less with a size distribution of 30–180 nm, an average size of 110 nm (Fig. 4 a), a weak SPR absorption peak at 535 nm and half peak width of 110 nm. When Na+ was added, the CDCa was released and the catalysis enhanced, resulting in more AuNPs in the analytical system. For the 8.6 μmol/L Na+ and 17.2 μmol/L Na+ analytical systems, the produced AuNPs size distributions were 30–300 nm and 20–170 nm, with an average size of 150 nm (Fig. 4b) and 130 nm (Fig. 4c) respectively. And both the SPR absorption peaks appeared at 540 nm and 550 nm with the half peak width of 105 and 90 nm respectively that indicated the formed AuNPs cause the SPR absorption band to shift-red. When the added Na+ increased, the average size of the formed AuNPs decreased due to the CDCa catalysis enhancing to form more Au seeds.

The particle size distribution of analytical system was tested by laser scattering. In the blank, the average particle size of the system was 234 nm, due to being of AuNPs aggregation in the system (Fig. S3a). With the increase of the Na+, the size of the produced gold nanoparticles became larger and the average particle size was 285 nm and 422 nm, respectively (Fig. S3b, S3c). The sizes of laser scattering were different to the SEM, the main reason is the AuNPs aggregation in the system.

The analytical conditions were optimized respectively (Fig. S4). The effect of GONRs concentration on the system was investigated and the ΔI values increased rapidly at first and reached the maximum when the concentration of CDCa was 0.67 ng/mL. So, 0.67 ng/mL CDCa was chosen. The ΔI values of SERS increased rapidly at first and decreased after the maximum with PA concentration increased. According to the result, 3.14 μmol/L PA was chosen·H2O2 was a reducer for the redox reaction and when the concentration of H2O2 was 3.3 mmol/L, ΔI was the largest. So 3.3 mmol/L H2O2 was chosen. The effect of HAuCl4 concentration was investigated according to the procedure and when HAuCl4 concentration was 0.193 mmol/L, the ΔI was the largest. So 0.193 mmol/L HAuCl4 was selected. The effect of reaction temperature was investigated in the range of 20–80 °C water bath. The ΔI enhanced sharply with the temperature increasing at 40–60 °C and tended to be stable before and after that. So, a water bath of 60 °C was used. The effect of reaction time The effect of reaction time of Na+-PA-CDCa-HAuCl4-H2O2 system was investigated. The ΔI got the maximum and tended to be stable when the reaction time was 25 min. So, a reaction time of 25 min was chosen. VB4r and Vitoria blue B (VBB) can be used as molecular probes, the VB4r is more sensitive due to its adsorbing stronger to the AuNP surface, and was selected for use, and the optimal concentration was 0.33 μmol/L VB4r.

Under the selected conditions, the working curve was plotted according the relationship between Na+ concentration (C) and ΔI values. For the CDCa catalytic analytical system, the ΔI was linear to the Na+ concentration over the range of 0.004–0.043 μmol/L, with detection limit (DL) of 1.9 nmol/L that DL was calculated by 3 times standard deviation of the blank five detections, this system is most sensitive due to the CDCa with the strongest catalysis to amplify the signal mostly, and was selected for use (Table 2 ). The analytical characteristics was compared with that of some reported methods (Ma, 2015; Oliveira et al., 2009; Smith & Haider, 2016; Liu, 2012; Zhang et al., 2012; Wang, Yu, & Wang, 2014) (Table S1), this SERS method is one of most sensitive.

The interference of common coexistence on the determination of 17.2 nmol/L Na+ was investigated within a relative error of ±10%. Results showed that 1.72 μmol/L (100 times) Ba2+, Cr6+, Ni2+, Bi3+, NH4 +, SO4 2−, glutamic acid, aspartic acid, valine, phenylalanine, 80 times Zn2+, Ca2+, K+, 0.86 μmol/L (50 times) Fe3+, Mg2+, Al3+, Pb2+ and 0.172 μmol/L (10 times) Cu2+, Mn2+ had no interference on the SERS determination of Na+. Therefore, the SERS quantitative analysis method had good selectivity.

According to the test method, Na+ was detected in the three types of drinking water from market, the Na content in water sample was 1.50 mg/L, 0.96 mg/L and 1.80 mg/L. A 395.6 ng/mL Na+ was added in the three tube containing sample, and operated according to the Section 2.4 Procedure to obtain 549.7, 581.9 and 469.2 ng/mL Na+. Then the recovery was calculated by (Found value – Average value)/Added value. The recovery was 96.0%–100.2% and a relative standard deviation (RSD) of 4.7%–5.1% (Table S2) were obtained, this indicated that the SERS quantitative analysis method is reliable, accuracy and repeatable.

Using the selective reaction of Na+-PA to control the CD catalysis of reduction of HAuCl4 with H2O2 to produce gold nanoparticles as SERS substrate, the VB4r molecular probe exhibited strong SERS effect at 1615 cm−1. The intensity of the SERS peak at 1615 cm−1 is increased linearly with analyte Na+ increasing. Thus, a new SERS quantitative analysis method was developed for the determination of Na+, with simplicity, high sensitivity and good selectivity. Indeed, most SERS studies were focused on the qualitative detection. Recently, it is interesting to SERS quantitative analytical method. In the past our studies, SERS quantitative analytical method with linear working curve is simple, accurate, reliable and repeatable, using repeatable nanosol as SERS substrate, and rapid scanning to record the spectra by this type of Raman spectrometry.
There is no conflict of interest.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: This work was supported by the National Key R&D Program of China (2018YFD0901003), Natural Science Foundation of Guangxi (No. 2018GXNSFAA138019) and National Natural Science Foundation of China (Nos. 21667006, 21767004).

ce:section.ce:label: Appendix A
ce:section.ce:para: Supplementary data to this article can be found online at https://doi.org/10.1016/j.foodchem.2019.03.032.

ce:section.ce:label: Appendix A
ce:section.ce:para:
The following are the Supplementary data to this article:
